reason: Global TianChen-RV architecture, RVV stage order, and route authority invariants for the dequant-clamp epilogue foundation.
RVV plugin-owned typed body, selected-body realization, route-provider, provider preflight, and evidence rules.
Common EmitC neutrality, provider-built TCRVEmitCLowerableRoute, conversion/dequant contracts, and target mirror validation.
Required lit/FileCheck, target artifact, generated-bundle, and ssh RVV evidence contracts.
Prior clamp/select provider/header mirror closure that this route builds on.
Prior executable ABI evidence pattern for f32 clamp/select bounds, counts, scalar oracle, and tail preservation.
Prior construction-protocol alignment for dequant-clamp route mnemonic without treating construction metadata as route authority.
Current task goal, boundaries, acceptance criteria, non-goals, and evidence plan.
Confirmed typed dequant-clamp f32 epilogue pre-realized body surface exists with lhs/scale/lower/upper/out/n roles and typed config attrs.
Confirmed verifier fail-closes stale op kind, memory form, dequant relation, scale role, bounds, ABI roles, C types, SEW, LMUL, and policy for the pre-realized body.
Confirmed RVV plugin-local selected-body realization emits setvl/load/dequantize/splats/two compare-select stages/store and erases the pre-realized op.

Confirmed provider planning derives dequant-clamp route-family facts, operand binding, dequant convert/scale facts, clamp bounds, result layout, headers, and C type mapping from the realized typed body.
Confirmed provider preflight gates dequant-clamp through compare/select and dequant contracts before building the lowerable route.
Confirmed target artifact validation mirrors provider-owned route/header/type/intrinsic/bound/dequant facts and fail-closes stale target metadata.
Confirmed generated-bundle dry-run supports dequant_clamp_f32_epilogue op-kind and emits a bundle evidence directory.

Added focused positive route/header assertions and negative stale-mirror target artifact checks for dequant scale, clamp bounds, operand binding, route family, headers, C types, intrinsics, ABI order, and provider mirror.
